{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: {#exr-prac-gauss-iid-lik}
#### Gaussian log-likelihood (adapted from @kleinbaum2014applied, Chapter 5)

Let $X_1, \ldots, X_n \siid \Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$.

**(a)** Write the likelihood $\Lik(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)$
for the observed data $\vx = (x_1, \ldots, x_n)$.

**(b)** Write the log-likelihood $\ell(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)$.
Show that it can be written as

$$
\ell(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)
= -\frac{n}{2}\logf{2\pi\sigma^2}
  - \frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2.
$$

**(c)** Derive the MLE $\hat\mu_{ML}$ and $\hat\sigma^2_{ML}$.
:::

::: {.solution}

**(a)**

$$
\Lik(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)
= \prod_{i=1}^n (2\pi\sigma^2)^{-1/2}
  \expf{-\frac{(x_i - \mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}}
= (2\pi\sigma^2)^{-n/2}
  \expf{-\frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i-\mu)^2}
$$

**(b)**

$$
\ba
\ell(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)
&= \logf{\Lik(\mu, \sigma^2; \vx)}
\\
&= -\frac{n}{2}\logf{2\pi\sigma^2}
   - \frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2
\ea
$$

**(c)**

**Deriving $\hat\mu_{ML}$:**

$$
\ba
\deriv{\mu}\ell
&= \deriv{\mu}\sb{-\frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2}
\\
&= \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)
\\
&= \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\paren{\sum_{i=1}^n x_i - n\mu}
\ea
$$

Setting this to zero: $\hat\mu_{ML} = \bar{x} \eqdef \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n x_i$.

**Deriving $\hat\sigma^2_{ML}$:**

$$
\ba
\deriv{\sigma^2}\ell
&= -\frac{n}{2\sigma^2} + \frac{1}{2(\sigma^2)^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2
\ea
$$

Setting this to zero and solving:

$$
\ba
\frac{n}{2\sigma^2}
&= \frac{1}{2(\sigma^2)^2}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2
\\
\sigma^2
&= \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \mu)^2
\ea
$$

Substituting $\hat\mu_{ML} = \bar{x}$:

$$
\hat\sigma^2_{ML} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \bar{x})^2
$$

Note: this is a biased estimator of $\sigma^2$;
the unbiased sample variance divides by $n-1$.

:::
